data.body:
PASS 1 review (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): no findings.

The T8 overlay applies only when a plan defines an LLM/agent system (prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, output schemas, enums). Cluster 1 is a byte-preserving DRY refactor of canonical-JSON/content-hash encoders onto the `_store/canonical.py` seam. It introduces no prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, output schemas, enums, decision rules, counters, or sub-step fallbacks. The one LLM-adjacent file it touches (`llm/workflow/schema.py`) is touched only at its `json.dumps`/`sha256` utility seam (`canonical_json`/`content_hash`), with the public names preserved as re-exports — verified against the live file: these are pure serialization/hashing helpers, not value vocabulary, protocol, or enum definitions. No structural-gap signal for this overlay.

Affirmed against the T8 checklist:
- No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined (the plan defines no enums; the workflow DSL's `step_kind`/discriminators are out of scope and untouched).
- No orphaned processing protocol/decision rule (none introduced).
- No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement (none introduced).
- No sub-step requiring a fallback (none introduced).
- No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern (no prompt text in scope); the one referenced LLM-adjacent symbol set (`schema.canonical_json`/`schema.content_hash`) exists and is fully specified (tool-verified in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/schema.py:390-403`).
